g.text: T M a — transformer T adds one effect on top of monad M
ReaderT r (ExceptT e (Writer w)) a — three-layer example
lift :: M a -> T M a

div.p: A monad transformer T turns a base monad M into a new monad T M that adds exactly one extra effect.
Stacking n transformers produces a type that carries n effects: T1 (T2 (T3 Base)) a
lift promotes an action from the layer below into the current layer. liftIO lifts any IO action to the top of an arbitrary stack.

g.text: ExceptT e — may fail with error e
innermost layer runs first when unwinding; outermost when binding

g.title: lift crosses one layer boundary. Use lift twice for two boundaries, or liftIO for any depth to reach IO.
lift crosses one layer boundary. Use lift twice for two boundaries, or liftIO for any depth to reach IO.
